seoTitle: How to Create a Resume Using Google Docs Templates
metaDescription: Build a professional resume fast using Google Docs' built-in template gallery, then export it as a polished PDF ready to send.
intro: Starting a resume from a blank page can be slow and frustrating when formatting keeps breaking. This guide shows how to use Google Docs' built-in template gallery to pick a professional layout, fill in your details, and export a polished PDF.

steps.heading: Open the Template Gallery
steps.body: In Google Docs, click the Template Gallery dropdown in the top right corner of the start screen.

steps.heading: Choose a resume layout
steps.body: Scroll to the Resumes section and select a clean, professional design that matches your industry.

steps.heading: Rename your document
steps.body: Click the document title in the top left and give the file a clear, searchable name.

steps.heading: Replace placeholder text
steps.body: Overwrite the header name, contact information, work experience, and education sections with your own details.

steps.heading: Export as PDF
steps.body: Click File, hover over Download, and select PDF Document to save a shareable version ready for job applications.

faq.question: Are Google Docs resume templates ATS-friendly?
faq.answer: Most built-in templates use standard formatting that works reasonably well with applicant tracking systems, though heavily designed layouts can cause issues.

faq.question: Can I change the template's fonts and colors?
faq.answer: Yes, you can select any text and adjust font, size, and color the same as in any other document.

faq.question: Should I export as PDF or Word?
faq.answer: PDF preserves formatting exactly as designed, while Word format is useful if an employer specifically requests a .docx file.

faq.question: Can I add a LinkedIn link to my resume header?
faq.answer: Yes, highlight your contact text and use the link tool to attach your LinkedIn profile URL.
